behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is the CLI’s interaction with an actual git repo and rename detection as produced by real git commands. A mocked diff/status stream can miss rename-classification quirks and subprocess/output handling differences that appear in CI/dev environments.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 add/0 delete for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures commonly occur at real subprocess/stdout decoding and real git diff behavior boundaries. Mocked text fixtures can hide encoding/format edge cases from actual git output.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI as a subprocess, assert non-crash exit and binary file included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure depends on real git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (exit codes/messages) and CLI subprocess error handling. Mocking git responses can hide real command semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, execute the CLI process, assert successful exit and empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real git operations over commits and file transitions. In-process fakes reduce confidence and can miss command/output mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with added/deleted/renamed/modified cases across commits, run CLI, and assert report counts equal ground-truth history computed from the repo scenario.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/worktree behaviors exposed through real git commands and filesystem state. Mocking usually hides index/worktree edge behavior and subprocess differences.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, prepare equivalent staged and unstaged change sets in separate runs, execute the CLI process for each, and assert identical structured stdout output.
